title: Dual-clock recurrent-event model instead of plain binary next-lap classification
summary: Pit stops are repeated events, and recurrent-event literature emphasizes modeling both total time and time since the previous event.
rationale: Pit stops are repeated events, and recurrent-event literature emphasizes modeling both total time and time since the previous event. Here, `LapNumber` and `TyreLife` naturally provide those two clocks, which may be more faithful than a flat classifier when risk accelerates near the end of a stint.
implementation_hint: Build a second model that predicts stop timing as a recurrent event using elapsed race time and elapsed stint time jointly. Practical version: train an XGBoost AFT or discrete-time survival surrogate on laps-to-next-pit within each stint, then convert to next-lap hazard and blend with the baseline classifier.
expected_effect: Sharper pit-window timing, especially for separating medium-risk laps from the final 1-2 laps before a stop.
risk: Survival labels and censoring logic are easy to get wrong; objective mismatch can hurt ROC AUC if the hazard conversion is noisy.
